# computer-networking

## Goal — the only thing the learner writes
- Why I'm learning this: moving toward a platform/SRE role — I keep hitting networking walls when debugging.
- Target tier: proficient

## Where you stand — written FOR you by the diagnostic
<!-- Generated by explore-gaps from real probes, not self-reported. -->
<!-- DIAGNOSIS:START -->
**🟢 Proven** — climbed to mechanism / trade-off
- IP address types — knows IPv4/IPv6 and public vs private, reasoned about NAT cleanly.

**🟡 Think you know** — felt familiar, fell off under a real question
- TCP vs UDP — "one is reliable" holds, but fell off on *why* (no model of ordering/retransmit/handshake cost). Gap: the mechanism behind reliability.
- DNS resolution — names the pieces, but couldn't walk the recursive resolve step by step.

**🔴 Blank** — surfaced by the map, not by the learner
- Subnetting and CIDR notation — confused; can't compute a range.
- The TCP three-way handshake — never named it.
- The OSI model layers — heard of it, can't name or place the layers.
<!-- DIAGNOSIS:END -->
